Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Boomloom: Think with your hands 144 upvotes, 15 comments
  2. My first patch to the Linux kernel 146 upvotes, 22 comments
  3. Sashiko: An agentic Linux kernel code review system 36 upvotes, 3 comments
  4. Electronics for Kids, 2nd Edition 217 upvotes, 46 comments
  5. Some things just take time 755 upvotes, 240 comments
  6. Bayesian statistics for confused data scientists 149 upvotes, 40 comments
  7. Floci – A free, open-source local AWS emulator 226 upvotes, 66 comments
  8. Professional video editing, right in the browser with WebGPU and WASM 303 upvotes, 107 comments
  9. 25 Years of Eggs 94 upvotes, 30 comments
  10. Flash-Moe: Running a 397B Parameter Model on a Mac with 48GB RAM 91 upvotes, 29 comments
  11. Hide macOS Tahoe's Menu Icons 241 upvotes, 96 comments
  12. Node.js worker threads are problematic, but they work great for us 15 upvotes, 2 comments
  13. It's Their Mona Lisa 49 upvotes, 15 comments
  14. HopTab–free,open source macOS app switcher and tiler that replaces Cmd+Tab 56 upvotes, 18 comments
  15. Hormuz Minesweeper – Are you tired of winning? 376 upvotes, 201 comments
  16. Bored of eating your own dogfood? Try smelling your own farts 6 upvotes, 0 comments
  17. Tinybox – A powerful computer for deep learning 528 upvotes, 298 comments
  18. The three pillars of JavaScript bloat 373 upvotes, 213 comments
  19. 'Miracle': Europe reconnects with lost spacecraft 53 upvotes, 22 comments
  20. More common mistakes to avoid when creating system architecture diagrams 16 upvotes, 4 comments
  21. Chest Fridge (2009) 135 upvotes, 76 comments
  22. Cross-Model Void Convergence: GPT-5.2 and Claude Opus 4.6 Deterministic Silence 40 upvotes, 21 comments
  23. A Review of Dice That Came with the White Castle 7 upvotes, 1 comments
  24. Brute-Forcing My Algorithmic Ignorance with an LLM in 7 Days 4 upvotes, 0 comments
  25. Project Nomad – Knowledge That Never Goes Offline 4 upvotes, 0 comments
  26. Show HN: Oku – One tab to filter out noise from feeds and content sources 11 upvotes, 4 comments
  27. Vatican Rebukes Peter Thiel's Antichrist Lectures in Rome 48 upvotes, 35 comments
  28. Windows native app development is a mess 48 upvotes, 45 comments
  29. $ teebot.dev – from terminal to tee in 6 seconds 4 upvotes, 2 comments
  30. How We Synchronized Editing for Rec Room's Multiplayer Scripting System 4 upvotes, 4 comments

Last generated 9:30pm, 22 March 2026. Source here.
